name: Alabama
tag: 5% top income tax; no estate or inheritance tax
cgn: Top 5% rate; no preferential capital gains treatment.
rtl: Social Security and pensions exempt; IRA/401(k) partly
rtn: SS and defined-benefit pension income fully exempt; first $6,000 of IRA/401(k) withdrawals exempt at 65+ (rises to $12,000 in 2026).
trn: Resident trusts taxed on all accumulated income; testator/grantor domicile is a residency basis.
qsn: No §1202 analog; federally excluded gain is fully taxed at up to 5%

name: Alaska
tag: No income, estate, or inheritance tax
cgn: No individual income tax, so no tax on capital gains.
rtn: No individual income tax, so Social Security, pensions and IRA/401(k) distributions are entirely untaxed.
trn: Premier situs: no income tax, self-settled asset protection trusts, 1,000-year perpetuities window.
qsn: No individual income tax, so the federal exclusion question is moot

name: Arizona
tag: Flat 2.5% income tax; no death tax
cgn: 2.5% flat rate less a 25% subtraction for net LTCG on assets acquired after 12/31/2011.
rtn: Social Security exempt, but private pensions, IRA and 401(k) income face the 2.5% flat tax; only a $2,500 subtraction for AZ/federal government pensions.
trn: Residency keys off the trustee/fiduciary's location, so changing trustees can change the tax.
qsn: Rolling IRC conformity; excluded gain never enters the 2.5% flat base

name: Arkansas
tag: 3.9% top income tax (cut for 2025); no death tax
cgn: Half of net LTCG is exempt against the 3.9% top rate; gains above $10M are fully exempt.
rtn: SS fully exempt; up to $6,000 of employer pension/401(k) or IRA distributions excluded at age 59.5+ (military pensions fully exempt).
trn: Resident trusts taxed on undistributed income at the graduated individual rates.
qsn: Specifically adopts §1202 under Ark. Code §26-51-815 despite its own base calculation

name: California
tag: 13.3% top income tax (incl. 1% surtax over $1M); no estate tax
cgn: Highest in the nation: 12.3% top bracket plus the 1% Mental Health Services surcharge above $1M.
rtn: Social Security exempt, but pensions, IRA and 401(k) distributions are fully taxed at rates up to 13.3% with no retirement exclusion.
trn: Highest in the nation; taxed on residence of fiduciaries and noncontingent beneficiaries, apportioned — settlor residence is irrelevant.
qsn: R&TC §18152 makes §1202 inapplicable; gain taxed at up to 13.3% plus the 1% surcharge

name: Colorado
tag: Flat 4.4% income tax; no death tax
cgn: 4.4% flat rate; narrow subtraction for Colorado agricultural land held 5+ years, capped at $100k.
rtl: Taxes SS with age-based deduction; $24k pension subtraction
rtn: SS fully deductible at 65+, and at 55-64 for 2025 if AGI is under $75,000 single/$95,000 joint; pension-annuity subtraction of $24,000 (65+) or $20,000 (55-64).
trn: Residency turns on where the trust is administered.
qsn: Rolling conformity from federal taxable income; excluded gain flows through

name: Connecticut
tag: Estate tax matches federal $13.99M exemption; flat 12%
cgn: Top 6.99% rate applies to gains with no preferential treatment.
rtl: Taxes SS above AGI thresholds; generous phase-ins
rtn: At most 25% of federally taxable SS is taxed; pension/annuity 100% exempt below $75,000 single/$100,000 joint AGI; IRA income 75% exempt in 2025, 100% in 2026.
trn: Inter vivos trusts are resident based solely on the grantor's domicile when funded; limited relief for nonresident beneficiaries.
qsn: Rolling conformity starting from federal AGI

name: Delaware
tag: 6.6% top income tax; no death tax (estate tax repealed 2018)
cgn: Top 6.6% rate; no capital gains exclusion.
rtn: SS exempt; up to $12,500 of pension and eligible retirement income excluded at age 60+ ($2,000 under 60), plus an extra standard deduction for seniors.
trn: Statutory 6.60% rate, but 30 Del. C. sec. 1636 fully deducts income accumulated for nonresident beneficiaries — a zero-tax situs when no Delaware beneficiary exists.
qsn: Rolling conformity starting from federal AGI

name: District of Columbia
tag: 10.75% income top; estate tax ~$4.87M exemption, 16%
cgn: Top 10.75% bracket applies to taxable income above $1M.
rtn: Social Security exempt, but pensions, IRA and 401(k) income are fully taxed at 4%-10.75%; only a $3,000 deduction for DC/federal government and military retirement at 62+.
trn: Among the highest fiduciary rates; resident trust status follows the grantor's or testator's domicile.
qsn: Council decoupled retroactive to 1/1/2025, adding back §1202(a) gain at up to 10.75%; contested after congressional disapproval

name: Florida
tag: No income, estate, or inheritance tax
cgn: No individual income tax, so no tax on capital gains.
rtn: No individual income tax and no estate tax; all retirement income is untaxed at the state level.
trn: No state income tax of any kind; constitutional bar on a personal income tax.
qsn: No individual income tax on wages or capital gains

name: Georgia
tag: Flat 5.19% income tax (accelerated cut for 2025); no death tax
cgn: HB 111 cut the flat rate from 5.39% to 5.19%, retroactive to January 1, 2025.
rtn: SS exempt; retirement income exclusion of $35,000 (ages 62-64 or disabled) and $65,000 (65+), per person, covering pensions, IRA/401(k), interest, dividends and capital gains.
trn: HB 111 cut the rate from 5.39% retroactive to 1/1/2025; beneficiary residence is a factor.
qsn: Static conformity updated to the IRC as of 1/1/2025, covering pre-OBBBA §1202

name: Hawaii
tag: 11% income top; estate tax $5.49M exemption, 20%
cgn: Long-term gains are capped at 7.25% versus an 11% top rate on ordinary income.
rtn: SS and employer-funded pension income fully exempt, but 401(k) elective deferrals, employee-contributed pension amounts and IRA distributions are taxable.
trn: Among the highest fiduciary rates; beneficiary residence is a residency factor.
qsn: HRS §235-2.45(e) turns off §1202(a)(3)–(4), capping the state exclusion at the original 50%

name: Idaho
tag: Flat 5.3% income tax (cut for 2025 via HB 40); no death tax
cgn: HB 40 cut the flat rate to 5.3% retroactive to 2025; a 60% deduction covers only qualified Idaho property, not securities.
rtn: SS exempt, but private pensions, IRA and 401(k) income face the 5.3% flat tax; the retirement benefits deduction covers only certain government/military pensions at 65+ (62+ if disabled).
trn: HB 40 cut the rate from 5.695% retroactive to 1/1/2025; trust property location is a residency basis.
qsn: Conformity date updated annually; federal exclusion flows through

name: Illinois
tag: Flat 4.95% income; estate tax $4M exemption, not portable
cgn: 4.95% flat rate with no capital gains preference.
rtn: Illinois exempts Social Security, pensions, IRA, 401(k) and other qualified plan distributions entirely despite a 4.95% flat tax on other income.
trn: 4.95% income tax plus a 1.5% personal property replacement tax that hits trusts but not estates; resident status follows the grantor's domicile alone.
qsn: Conforms for 2025; SB 3019 imposes a §1202 addback only for tax years ending on or after 12/31/2026

name: Indiana
tag: Flat 3% income tax (plus local add-ons); no death tax
cgn: 3.0% state flat rate; county income taxes apply on top.
rtn: SS exempt, but pensions, IRA and 401(k) income are fully taxed at the ~3.0% flat state rate plus county tax; only federal civil-service and military pensions get a deduction.
trn: Resident trusts taxed on accumulated income; trustee residence is a factor.
qsn: Annual static update starting from federal AGI; no §1202 addback

name: Iowa
tag: Flat 3.8% income; inheritance tax fully repealed for 2025
cgn: 3.8% flat rate, but qualified retired-farmer gains and employee-owned (ESOP) stock are 100% excluded.
rtn: SS exempt for all, and since 2023 all pension, IRA and 401(k) income is exempt for residents 55+, the disabled and qualifying surviving spouses.
trn: Flat tax as of 2025; trustee residence is a residency basis.
qsn: Static conformity to the IRC in effect for 2025; excluded gain is not added back

name: Kansas
tag: 5.58% top income tax; no death tax
cgn: Top rate of 5.58% under the two-bracket structure.
rtl: Private pensions/IRA fully taxed (SS and public pensions exempt)
rtn: SS fully exempt regardless of income since 2024, and KPERS/federal/military pensions are exempt, but private pensions, IRA and 401(k) distributions are fully taxable.
trn: Resident trusts taxed on undistributed income at graduated rates.
qsn: Rolling conformity starting from federal AGI

name: Kentucky
tag: Flat 4% income; inheritance tax up to 16% (Class A heirs exempt)
cgn: 4.0% flat rate for 2025, down from 4.5%.
rtn: SS exempt; up to $31,110 of pension, IRA and 401(k) income excluded per taxpayer at any age, with service before 1998 potentially fully exempt.
trn: Rate steps down to 3.5% in 2026; trustee residence is a factor.
qsn: Static conformity to the IRC as of 12/31/2024, which includes pre-OBBBA §1202

name: Louisiana
tag: Flat 3% income tax (consolidated for 2025); no death tax
cgn: New 3.0% flat rate replaced graduated brackets in 2025.
rtn: SS exempt; Louisiana and federal government pensions fully exempt; up to $12,000 of other annual retirement income excluded at 65+ for 2025 (doubled from $6,000, now CPI-indexed).
trn: Fiduciary income taxed at a flat 3% for periods beginning on or after 1/1/2025; grantor domicile creates residency.
qsn: Starts from federal AGI under the 2025 flat 3% structure

name: Maine
tag: 7.15% income top; estate tax ~$7M exemption, 12%
cgn: Top 7.15% rate; no capital gains exclusion.
rtn: SS exempt; pension and eligible retirement income deduction up to $48,216 per person for 2025, reduced by SS/Railroad benefits and newly subject to an income-based phaseout.
trn: Trust is resident if the grantor was a Maine resident — a settlor-domicile trap that survives the settlor's move.
qsn: Static conformity updated into 2025; no §1202 modification

name: Maryland
tag: Only state with BOTH estate ($5M) and inheritance tax; new 6.5% income top
cgn: New for 2025: two new brackets topping at 6.5% plus a 2% surcharge on net capital gains when federal AGI exceeds $350k; local tax adds up to 3.2%.
rtn: SS exempt; pension exclusion up to $41,200 for 2025 at 65+ or disabled, offset dollar-for-dollar by SS; traditional/Roth IRA, SEP and Keogh distributions do not qualify.
trn: New 6.25%/6.50% brackets apply retroactively to 2025; resident fiduciaries also owe county local income tax on top, and grantor domicile creates residency.
qsn: Rolling conformity; state and county piggyback tax both follow federal AGI

name: Massachusetts
tag: 9% income (5% + 4% surtax); estate tax $2M threshold, 16%
cgn: Long-term gains at 5% and short-term at 8.5%, each plus the 4% surtax on income above $1,083,150.
rtl: Private pensions/IRA fully taxed (SS and public pensions exempt)
rtn: SS and most federal/Massachusetts public pensions exempt, but private pensions, IRA and 401(k) income are taxed at 5%, plus the 4% surtax above roughly $1.08M.
trn: 5% base plus the 4% millionaire surtax, which expressly applies to trusts and estates above roughly $1.08M of taxable income.
qsn: Full 100% exclusion for sales on or after 1/1/2022 per TIR 23-5; any included QSBS gain is taxed at 3%

name: Michigan
tag: Flat 4.25% income tax; no death tax
cgn: 4.25% flat rate; some cities levy an additional local income tax.
rtl: Retirement deduction phasing in (75% tier in 2025)
rtn: SS exempt; 2025 is year three of the phase-in — taxpayers born 1946-1966 may deduct 75% of the retirement maximum ($49,423 single / $98,846 joint); full restoration in 2026.
trn: Resident trust status follows the grantor's domicile alone.
qsn: Rolling conformity from federal AGI at the 4.25% flat rate

name: Minnesota
tag: 9.85% income top; estate tax $3M exemption, 16%
cgn: Top 9.85% rate plus the 1% net investment income tax on investment income above $1M.
rtl: Taxes SS above income limits; pensions fully taxed
rtn: Full SS subtraction only if AGI is under $84,490 single / $108,320 joint for 2025, phasing out above that; pensions, IRA and 401(k) income are otherwise fully taxed.
trn: Grantor-domicile residency, though Fielding v. Commissioner limits it as applied where other Minnesota contacts are absent.
qsn: Static conformity covering pre-OBBBA §1202; excluded gain also escapes the 9.85% top rate

name: Mississippi
tag: Flat 4.4% income tax (phasing down); no death tax
cgn: 4.4% flat rate for 2025, stepping down in later years.
rtn: SS exempt, and qualified distributions from pensions, IRAs, 401(k)s, 403(b)s and 457(b)s are exempt; early (pre-59.5) withdrawals remain taxable.
trn: Residency keys off place of administration; rate drops to 4.0% in 2026.
qsn: State law contains no §1202 exclusion; gain is fully taxed at the flat rate

name: Missouri
tag: 4.7% top income tax (cut for 2025); no death tax
cgn: HB 594 allows individuals a 100% deduction for all federally reported capital gains, retroactive to January 1, 2025.
rtl: SS and public pensions exempt; $6,000 private pension break
rtn: SS fully exempt at 62+ since 2024; public pension deduction up to $47,633 for 2025 with no AGI cap; private pension deduction capped at $6,000 and income-tested.
trn: Trap: the 2025 law fully exempting capital gains applies only to individuals, not trusts and estates, so accumulated gains remain taxable.
qsn: Rolling conformity starting from federal AGI

name: Montana
tag: 5.9% top income tax; no death tax
cgn: Net long-term gains are taxed at 3.0% then 4.1%, versus a 5.9% top rate on ordinary income.
rtn: Montana taxes the same portion of SS as the IRS (repeal bill HB 148 died in May 2025); partial pension/annuity exemption of about $5,780 per person that phases out as AGI rises.
trn: Uses trustee residence, grantor domicile, and beneficiary residence as alternative bases; rate falls to 5.65% in 2026.
qsn: Rolling conformity; excluded gain stays out of the state base

name: Nebraska
tag: 5.2% income top; county inheritance tax up to 15% (close relatives 1%)
cgn: Top 5.2% rate; a one-time lifetime election excludes gains on employer stock.
rtn: SS fully exempt since 2024, but pensions, IRA and 401(k) distributions are fully taxed up to 4.55%; military retirement, CSRS annuities and Railroad benefits are exempt.
trn: Trustee residence is a residency basis; rate drops to 4.55% in 2026.
qsn: Rolling conformity starting from federal AGI

name: Nevada
tag: No income, estate, or inheritance tax
cgn: No individual income tax, so no tax on capital gains.
rtn: No individual income tax; Social Security, pensions and IRA/401(k) distributions are untaxed at the state level.
trn: Top-tier situs: no income tax, 365-year perpetuities period, strong self-settled spendthrift statute.
qsn: No individual income tax on wages or capital gains

name: New Hampshire
tag: No wage income tax (I&D tax repealed 1/1/2025); no death tax
cgn: Never taxed capital gains; the interest and dividends tax was fully repealed effective January 1, 2025.
rtn: The interest-and-dividends tax was fully repealed effective January 1, 2025, so New Hampshire now taxes no individual income, including all retirement income.
trn: The interest and dividends tax was repealed effective 1/1/2025, making New Hampshire fully tax-free for trusts.
qsn: Interest and dividends tax repealed effective 2025 and capital gains were never taxed

name: New Jersey
tag: 10.75% income top; inheritance tax up to 16% (Class A heirs exempt)
cgn: Top 10.75% rate on income above $1M.
rtl: Up to $100,000 exclusion, with a $150,000 cliff
rtn: SS exempt; at 62+ or disabled, exclude up to $100,000 (joint) / $75,000 (single) / $50,000 (MFS) of pension, annuity, IRA and 401(k) income — but it phases down above $100,000 and drops to zero above $150,000 of total income.
trn: Resident status follows grantor/testator domicile, but a safe harbor exempts trusts with no New Jersey trustee, assets, or source income.
qsn: P.L. 2025 c.67 (A4455) grants the exclusion only for dispositions on or after 1/1/2026; 2025 gain taxed up to 10.75%

name: New Mexico
tag: 5.9% top income tax (new bracket for 2025); no death tax
cgn: Top 5.9% rate; the 2025 deduction is the greater of $2,500 or 40% of up to $1M of gain from selling a New Mexico business.
rtl: Taxes SS above income limits; $8,000 senior deduction
rtn: SS exempt when AGI is under $100,000 single / $150,000 joint / $75,000 MFS; otherwise taxable. Separate $8,000 income-tested deduction at 65+; pensions and IRA/401(k) otherwise fully taxed.
trn: Residency determined by the trustee's location.
qsn: Starts from federal taxable income; a 40% capital gains deduction applies to non-excluded gain

name: New York
tag: 10.9% income top; estate tax 'cliff' above $7.16M, 16%
cgn: Top 10.9% rate above $25M; New York City residents owe an additional local tax.
rtn: SS exempt; New York state, local, federal and military pensions fully exempt; up to $20,000 per person of other pension, IRA and 401(k) income excluded at age 59.5+.
trn: Grantor-domicile residency, but the exempt resident trust safe harbor applies if no trustee is domiciled in NY, no NY situs assets, and no NY source income — all three must hold.
qsn: Follows the federal exclusion for both New York State and New York City purposes

name: North Carolina
tag: Flat 4.25% income tax (cut for 2025); no death tax
cgn: 4.25% flat rate for 2025, continuing a scheduled phase-down.
rtn: SS fully exempt, but pensions, IRA and 401(k) income are taxed at the 4.25% flat rate for 2025; only Bailey-settlement government retirees (vested by 8/12/1989) are exempt.
trn: Post-Kaestner, beneficiary residence alone cannot support taxation absent further in-state contacts.
qsn: Static conformity that incorporates pre-OBBBA §1202; no addback

name: North Dakota
tag: 2.5% top income tax (lowest graduated top rate); no death tax
cgn: 40% of qualifying long-term gains is excluded against a 2.5% top rate.
rtn: SS exempt since 2021, but pensions, IRA and 401(k) distributions are fully taxable — though North Dakota's top rate of 2.5% makes the burden among the lightest in the nation.
trn: Lowest rate among taxing states; trustee and beneficiary residence are factors.
qsn: Starts from federal taxable income; excluded gain flows through

name: Ohio
tag: 3.125% income top (flat 2.75% arrives 2026); no death tax
cgn: HB 96 cut the 2025 top rate from 3.5% to 3.125%; a flat 2.75% follows in 2026.
rtn: SS exempt, but public and private pensions, IRA and 401(k) income are fully taxable; relief is limited to a retirement income credit capped at $200 and a small senior citizen credit.
trn: HB 96 cut the top nonbusiness bracket from 3.5% retroactive to 2025; testator domicile creates resident trust status.
qsn: Rolling conformity starting from federal AGI

name: Oklahoma
tag: 4.75% top income tax; no death tax
cgn: Top 4.75% rate, but gains on Oklahoma property or Oklahoma company stock meeting holding periods are 100% deductible.
rtn: SS fully exempt; up to $10,000 per taxpayer of pension, IRA and 401(k) income excluded (military retirement 100% exempt). The exclusion rises to $40,000 beginning in 2026.
trn: Grantor/testator domicile is a residency basis for resident trusts.
qsn: Rolling conformity starting from federal AGI

name: Oregon
tag: 9.9% income top; estate tax at just $1M (not indexed), 16%
cgn: Top 9.9% rate; Portland-area residents face additional local income taxes.
rtn: SS exempt, but pensions, IRA and 401(k) income are taxed at rates up to 9.9%; the retirement income credit applies only at very low household income and expires after tax year 2025.
trn: Residency determined by the trustee's location — an out-of-state trustee generally avoids Oregon fiduciary tax.
qsn: Conforms for 2025; SB 1507 decouples beginning with 2026, taxing excluded gain at 9.9%

name: Pennsylvania
tag: Flat 3.07% income; inheritance tax up to 15%, no exemption (spouse 0%)
cgn: 3.07% flat rate; gains cannot be netted against other income classes and losses cannot be carried forward.
rtn: SS fully exempt, and pension, IRA, 401(k) and other qualified plan distributions are exempt from the 3.07% tax once you reach age 59.5 or normal retirement age; early withdrawals of earnings are taxable.
trn: Low flat rate, but resident trust status follows the settlor's domicile at creation.
qsn: Personal income tax does not incorporate Subchapter P; gain is fully taxed at 3.07%

name: Rhode Island
tag: 5.99% income top; low estate tax exemption $1,802,431, 16%
cgn: Top 5.99% rate; no capital gains exclusion.
rtl: Taxes SS above income limits; $20,000 pension exemption
rtn: At full retirement age with federal AGI under $107,000 single / $133,750 joint, SS is fully subtracted and up to $20,000 of pension/401(k)/403(b)/annuity income is exempt (raised from $15,000). IRA distributions do not qualify.
trn: Grantor/testator domicile creates resident trust status.
qsn: Rolling conformity starting from federal AGI

name: South Carolina
tag: 6.2% top income tax (cut for 2025); no death tax
cgn: 44% of net capital gain is deducted against the 6.2% top rate.
rtn: SS exempt; deduct up to $10,000 of qualifying retirement income at 65+ ($3,000 under 65), plus a separate $15,000 age-65 deduction against any income (reduced by the retirement deduction claimed).
trn: FY2026 budget temporarily cut the top rate from 6.2% to 6.0% for tax year 2025.
qsn: Conforms, and a 44% deduction applies to any long-term gain that is not excluded

name: South Dakota
tag: No income, estate, or inheritance tax
cgn: No individual income tax, so no tax on capital gains.
rtn: No individual income tax; Social Security, pensions and IRA/401(k) distributions are untaxed at the state level.
trn: The benchmark dynasty trust situs: no income tax, no rule against perpetuities, strongest privacy and directed trust statutes.
qsn: No individual income tax on wages or capital gains

name: Tennessee
tag: No income, estate, or inheritance tax (Hall tax repealed 2021)
cgn: No individual income tax; the Hall investment income tax was repealed in 2021.
rtn: The Hall tax on interest and dividends was fully repealed in 2021, so no retirement income of any kind is taxed.
trn: Hall interest and dividends tax fully repealed as of 2021; 360-year trusts, DAPT and silent trust statutes.
qsn: Hall tax on interest and dividends fully repealed; capital gains untaxed

name: Texas
tag: No income, estate, or inheritance tax
cgn: No individual income tax, so no tax on capital gains.
rtn: No individual income tax; all Social Security, pension and IRA/401(k) income escapes state tax, though property taxes are high.
trn: No state income tax on individuals or trusts; constitutional barrier to enactment.
qsn: No individual income tax on wages or capital gains

name: Utah
tag: Flat 4.55% income tax; no death tax
cgn: HB 106 cut the flat rate from 4.55% to 4.50%, retroactive to January 1, 2025.
rtl: Taxes SS with a phasing-out credit
rtn: SS is in the 4.5% flat tax base but offset by a nonrefundable credit that phases out above $54,000 single / $90,000 joint (thresholds raised by SB 71 in 2025); pensions and IRA/401(k) fully taxed apart from a $450 credit for those born on or before 12/31/1952.
trn: HB 106 cut the rate from 4.55% retroactive to 1/1/2025; residency keys off place of administration.
qsn: Rolling conformity from federal AGI at the 4.55% flat rate

name: Vermont
tag: 8.75% income top; estate tax $5M exemption, flat 16%
cgn: Top 8.75% rate; only a $5,000 flat exclusion applies to publicly traded stock, since the 40% exclusion excludes it.
rtl: Taxes SS above income limits; $10,000 exclusion
rtn: SS fully exempt below $55,000 single / $70,000 joint AGI for 2025 (thresholds raised), phasing out above that; up to $10,000 of CSRS or other retirement system income exempt, income-tested — only one exemption may be claimed.
trn: Grantor domicile creates resident trust status.
qsn: Static conformity incorporating pre-OBBBA §1202; no state addback

name: Virginia
tag: 5.75% top income tax; no death tax
cgn: Top 5.75% rate; no capital gains exclusion.
rtn: SS exempt; age deduction of up to $12,000 per person born on or before 1/1/1961, phased out dollar-for-dollar above $50,000 single / $75,000 joint AFAGI; $30,000 military benefit deduction for 2025.
trn: Taxes on both grantor domicile and trustee residence, giving the state two independent hooks.
qsn: Fixed-date conformity advanced into 2025, covering the pre-OBBBA exclusion

name: Washington
tag: No income tax; highest US estate tax 35% (from 7/1/2025), $3M exemption
cgn: No wage income tax; a 7% excise applies to long-term gains above the $278,000 standard deduction, plus 2.9% above $1M under SB 5813, effective 2025.
rtl: No income tax on retirement income
rtn: No individual income tax on wages or retirement income; the 7% capital gains excise tax applies to certain gains but not to pension, IRA or 401(k) distributions.
trn: The 7%–9.9% capital gains excise tax reaches individuals only; non-grantor trusts are exempt, though Washington beneficiaries may be taxed on distributed gains.
qsn: The 7% capital gains excise tax starts from federal net long-term gain, so §1202 gain is outside the base

name: West Virginia
tag: 4.82% top income tax (triggered cut for 2025); no death tax
cgn: Top rate fell to 4.82% for 2025 after triggered and legislated cuts.
rtl: SS 65% deductible in 2025, fully exempt in 2026
rtn: Phase-out year: 65% of Social Security is deductible for 2025 (35% in 2024, 100% in 2026). Separate $8,000 modification per person at 65+ or disabled; public pensions partially exempt.
trn: Grantor domicile and trustee residence are both residency bases.
qsn: Annual conformity update starting from federal AGI

name: Wisconsin
tag: 7.65% top income tax; no death tax
cgn: 30% of net long-term gain is excluded (60% for farm assets) against the 7.65% top rate.
rtn: SS exempt; starting in 2025, subtract up to $24,000 of qualified retirement income at age 67+ ($48,000 for joint filers where both are 67+); federal/military and certain WI public pensions are fully exempt.
trn: Resident status follows the testator's domicile or in-state administration.
qsn: 2023 Act 36 restored full §1202 conformity retroactive to 2019, ending the prior 50% cap

name: Wyoming
tag: No income, estate, or inheritance tax
cgn: No individual income tax, so no tax on capital gains.
rtn: No individual income tax and no estate tax; Social Security, pensions and IRA/401(k) distributions are untaxed at the state level.
trn: No income tax, 1,000-year perpetuities period, and strong asset protection and privacy statutes.
qsn: No individual income tax on wages or capital gains
